serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chicken kebab wrap weighs approximately 200 g and constitutes a complete single-serve meal providing around 476 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Kebab (Wrap) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken Kebab (Wrap), cr_id F05020026. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Flatbread / pita-based mixed meal analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken kebab wrap as a composite dish. GI of 57 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white pita bread / flatbread (GI ~57, medium) as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle. The protein (17.7 g/100g) and fat (9.9 g/100g) content of the wrap will attenuate the effective glycemic response below the flatbread-alone GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 19.4 × 2.0) / 100 ≈ 22.1; rounded to 21.5 accounting for fibre attenuation. This is a defensible analogous estimate per University of Sydney GI Database methodology.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 327 mg per 100 g; a 200 g serving delivers approximately 654 mg sodium, representing 28% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced or no added sauce (garlic sauce and chili sauce are the primary sodium contributors), avoid extra salt or pickled vegetables, and limit portion to one wrap per meal to keep sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (19.4 g/100g, GI ~57 medium) offset by high protein (17.7 g/100g) and dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g) which attenuate postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes can include a chicken kebab wrap as part of a balanced meal by choosing a wholemeal or high-fibre flatbread to lower the effective GI, limiting high-sugar sauces, and monitoring portion size to keep total carbohydrate per meal within their individualised target (typically 45–60 g per meal).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 327 mg/100g and estimated saturated fat ~2.8 g/100g are the primary cardiovascular risk factors; grilled preparation and lean chicken protein are favourable.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, choose a yogurt-based sauce over mayonnaise to reduce saturated fat, request reduced sauce to lower sodium, and pair the wrap with a salad rather than fried sides to keep the overall meal saturated fat and sodium within heart-healthy limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Grilled preparation avoids advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); anti-inflammatory spices in marinade; nightshade vegetables (tomato, chili) may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor their response to nightshade vegetables (tomato, chili pepper) in the wrap filling, request extra turmeric or ginger in the marinade for anti-inflammatory benefit, and avoid deep-fried chicken kebab variants.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Kebab Wrap is a popular composite dish consisting of grilled, spiced chicken pieces served inside a soft flatbread or wrap together with fresh vegetables such as tomato, cucumber, and lettuce, and finished with a garlic or chili sauce. The chicken is typically marinated in a blend of Middle Eastern spices including cumin, coriander, paprika, and garlic before being grilled or cooked on a vertical rotisserie, yielding a smoky, savoury flavour with a tender, slightly charred texture. The flatbread is soft and pliable, providing a neutral starchy base that absorbs the sauce and meat juices. Per 100 g, the dish provides 238 kcal, 17.7 g protein, 9.9 g fat, and 19.4 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, moderate-energy meal option. Dietary fibre is 1.8 g/100g and sodium is 327 mg/100g, with the sauce being the primary sodium contributor. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 57 (medium) based on the flatbread component, though the high protein and fat content attenuate the postprandial glucose response in practice. Clinically, the wrap is suitable for most adults as part of a balanced diet; individuals managing hypertension should request reduced sauce, and those with type 2 diabetes should prefer wholemeal flatbread and monitor portion size. The dish is widely available in Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and Middle Eastern fast-food outlets, and is considered halal. It is culturally associated with Middle Eastern cuisine but has been fully integrated into the Singaporean multicultural food landscape.

confidence_reason: Core nutritional values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020026) at confidence 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (flatbread/pita, GI ~57) which is the approved GI source; confidence is medium for the GI component but high overall for the nutrient profile.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at 2.8 g/100g based on typical grilled chicken wrap composition from USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy, Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, roasted) as a supplementary reference; the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a matched composite entry as of 2026. 3. GI value of 57 is an analogous estimate based on white pita/flatbread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; the actual GI of the assembled wrap will be lower due to protein and fat attenuation. 4. Dietary tags in the input included 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' which are incorrect for a chicken-containing dish and have been removed. 5. The input occasion tag 'breakfast' has been replaced with more appropriate meal contexts (lunch, dinner, supper) consistent with typical consumption patterns for this dish in Singapore. 6. FoodEx2 match type corrected from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].
